data.title: Washington's "Coming of Age": A Deep Dive into the Clarity Act
data.description:
Author: Changan, Editor: Denise

In early 2026, after five years of regulatory wrangling and hundreds of enforcement cases, the global cryptocurrency market's attention was focused on Capitol Hill in Washington. This bill, named CLARITY, was originally intended to provide clarity for digital assets that had long been in a regulatory gray area, but at the last minute it evolved into an ultimate battle between the old and new financial orders.

On January 17, the White House announced it might consider withdrawing its support, pushing the CLARITY bill back into the spotlight.

Today, we delve into this hundreds-of-pages bill, not to delve into the legal statutes, but to explore: Why did Coinbase, which had initially spearheaded the embrace of regulation, "turn against" at the last minute? And how will this stack of hundreds of pages change your wallet, as a retail investor?

01 

02 Bald Brother's "Midnight Rebellion"

The original version of the CLARITY Act had a clear intention: it attempted to redefine the rules through three pillars: asset classification, financing regulation, and stablecoin access.

The initial version of the CLARITY Act had a clear intention: it attempted to redefine the rules through three pillars: asset classification, financing regulation, and stablecoin access.

Institutions: Compliance Dividend

For institutions, this is more like a long-awaited compliance ticket. Legal certainty is a prerequisite for giants like Goldman Sachs and BlackRock to enter the market. Once the jurisdictional boundaries between the SEC and CFTC are clarified, billions of dollars of institutional funds will be compliantly allocated to digital commodities beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum, which will inevitably trigger a wave of applications for altcoin spot ETFs.

Project Teams: A Mixed Bag of Joy and Sorrow Projects defined as digital goods are now free from the SEC's scrutiny; those defined as securities face extremely heavy compliance reporting obligations and financing restrictions. Furthermore, the bill mandates a lock-up period for core team tokens, effectively curbing the bad habit of dumping tokens at the opening. Fortunately, the bill explicitly protects non-custodial developers. If you only write code and release open-source licenses without handling customer funds, you will not be considered a money transmitter, protecting pure technical innovation at the protocol level.

04 Industry Debate: Consensus or Division?

Biteye has compiled the positions of KOLs and project teams in the industry on the latest revised bill.

Biteye has compiled the positions of KOLs and project teams in the industry on the latest revised bill.

AB Kuai.Dong @_FORAB (XHunt ranking: 1087)

Opinion: Reports on Coinbase's sudden defection suggest that the latest version of the bill is friendly to traditional banks but unfavorable to crypto-native companies.

qinbafrank @qinbafrank (XHunt ranking: 1533)

Opinion: The Senate Banking Committee's decision to cancel its review due to Coinbase's opposition could lead to a market correction. The focus of the opposition includes a "de facto ban" on tokenized equity, DeFi privacy violations, weakening the CFTC's power, and eliminating stablecoin rewards, which they argue would allow the SEC to dominate and stifle innovation.

Phyrex@Phyrex_Ni (XHunt Ranking: 765)

Opinion: Analyzed the reasons why the Coinbsase CEO blocked the bill, including restrictions on tokenized stocks, functional regulation of DeFi, the issue of the SEC's power boundaries, the prohibition of interest-bearing stablecoins, and the ethical conflict of interest of the Trump family.

PANews@PANews (XHunt Ranking: 1827) Opinion: The view is that delays will become increasingly detrimental if the bill is delayed. January is one of the few available structural legislative windows in the Senate; if substantial progress is not made, it could easily be "naturally squeezed out" of the overall legislative schedule. Meanwhile, if the Democrats gain an advantage in the midterm elections, the probability of passage will be even lower. Chen Jian Jason @jason_chen998 (XHunt ranking: 1082) Opinion: The conflict is essentially driven by the interests of each party. For example, Coinbase publicly opposes it because the current version's ban on issuing interest on stablecoins would directly lead to Coinbase losing $1 billion in revenue annually and a large number of users. Ripple's CEO, on the other hand, strongly supports the Clarity Act, also because the ban on issuing interest on stablecoins has little impact on Ripple. Bitcoin Orange @chengzi_95330 (XHunt Ranking: 3508) Opinion: While the current plan is imperfect, a16z, Circle, Kraken, and others are willing to continue pushing forward because they fear that if they overturn the table now, the legislative window may close directly. Coinbase believes that if core issues such as stablecoin yields cannot be addressed in such a crypto-friendly political environment, then there will be no chance for this in a future, more anti-crypto political cycle. Therefore, they are making a "bet on historical judgment." Brad Garlinghouse (Ripple CEO) @bgarlinghouse (XHunt Ranking: 1870) Opinion: Surprised by Coinbase's strong opposition, Garlinghouse believes Brian's concerns are reasonable, but emphasizes that "the rest of the industry is still constructively supportive and working to resolve the issues." Garlinghouse stated that Ripple is prepared to move forward within a compliant framework (such as XRPL tokenization), views the legislation as a step forward, and is unwilling to abandon the overall process due to disagreements. Vlad Tenev (Robinhood CEO) @vladtenev (XHunt Ranking: 380) Opinion: Supporting progress. He reiterated Robinhood's support for Congress passing the Market Structure Act, acknowledging that work remains to be done (such as addressing staking restrictions in some states and the availability of stock tokenization), but seeing a clear path and being willing to assist the Senate Banking Committee in completing it. He emphasized that the US needs to lead crypto policy to unlock innovation and protect consumers. Arjun Sethi (Kraken co-CEO) @arjunsethi (XHunt ranking: 1941) Opinion: Strongly supportive. He stated that Kraken is fully committed to supporting the efforts of Tim Scott and Cynthia Lummis, criticizing the ease with which they "walk away or declare defeat," but emphasizing that what truly matters is "remaining present, solving problems, and building consensus." He warned that giving up would exacerbate uncertainty and drive innovation overseas.

05 Retail Investors' Risk Aversion and Gold Rush: A Guide to Action in 2026

A coming-of-age ceremony, a new beginning.

A coming-of-age ceremony, a new beginning.

1. Reassess the portfolio and shift towards "digital commodities" assets

For crypto asset holdings, increase the allocation weight of assets explicitly classified as "digital commodities" (such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.) and mature blue-chip tokens in their ecosystems. These assets will first see a large influx of compliant funds from traditional institutions due to the elimination of regulatory uncertainty, and their spot ETFs and other products will be easier to obtain approval, thus forming strong price support. Conversely, extreme caution should be exercised with newly issued tokens that are clearly likely to be classified as "securities," as they will face strict disclosure and financing restrictions, and liquidity may dry up.

1. Reassess the portfolio and shift towards "digital commodities" assets

For crypto asset holdings, increase the allocation weight of assets explicitly classified as "digital commodities" (such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.) and mature blue-chip tokens in their ecosystems. These assets will first see a large influx of compliant funds from traditional institutions due to the elimination of regulatory uncertainty, and their spot ETFs and other products will be easier to obtain approval, thus forming strong price support.

1. Reassess the portfolio and shift towards "digital commodities" assets

data.title: Why is the Fed's massive quantitative easing the most fatal blow to the dollar in the new currency war?
data.description:
Author: Rick awsb; Source: X,@rickawsb

“The size of the Federal Reserve’s balance sheet may need to grow in the long run along with the growth of the economy and the financial system.”

This is a signal that has been repeatedly released by several Federal Reserve officials recently.

It can be said to be unprecedented in the history of the Fed—this is significantly different from the past cyclical narrative of “expansion-shrinkage-return to normal”.

This may be one of a series of actions by the Fed in response to the long-term impact of the AI ​​revolution.

AI is not bringing about a new industrial cycle, but rather a superposition of multiple (Kondratiev) cycles. In an era of extremely high capital density, highly irreversible infrastructure investment, and potentially enormous returns, the real systemic risk does not stem from short-term inflation fluctuations, but from disruptions. Once computing power, energy, networks, and data centers are forced to stop, the costs are exponential and often irreparable. The essence of balance sheet expansion is using the existing system's credit to expedite long-term investments that cannot wait. The potential effects of these AI-related long-term investments will ultimately determine the fate of nations (if nations still exist in the post-AI era), and thus, the fate of fiat currencies. Therefore, the long-term expansion of the US dollar's balance sheet may be the most important blow in the current global currency war. This, of course, is not without cost. Expanding the balance sheet internally is essentially trading credit for stability. "It inevitably dilutes the credibility of the dollar," but focusing solely on this aspect leads to a misjudgment of what is happening. If understood using the Bagehot principle, what the Federal Reserve is doing is not unfamiliar. The traditional "lender of last resort" provides support to institutions temporarily lacking liquidity during a crisis, preventing localized problems from escalating into systemic collapse. The difference in the Fed's approach today is that it's not taking over after a crisis erupts, but preventing it before it occurs: AI, energy, computing power, and infrastructure—these new production functions share the common characteristics of extremely high capital intensity, highly upfront investment, and the near irreversible impact of any disruption. If the central bank intervenes only after the "crisis" manifests in its traditional form, it is often too late. Therefore, the Fed's role as lender of last resort has been artificially brought forward. But the problem lies precisely here: If the Fed assumes this pre-emptive and normalized role of lender of last resort for a long time, will the credibility of the dollar be continuously eroded? The answer is obvious. However, the US solution, as we've discussed before, is to **strengthen internal stability through external pressures, embrace cryptocurrencies, bypass financial regulations in various countries, and maintain the dollar's status through global financial colonization**. Doesn't this change in the US attitude towards cryptocurrencies seem more reasonable within the context of the AI ​​era? This creates a seemingly contradictory but logically consistent structure: on one hand, expanding its balance sheet domestically, assuming the role of lender of last resort, and stabilizing the banking system, the Treasury market, and financial infrastructure; on the other hand, continuously expanding the dollar's reach in new markets through cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, and new financial models. Within the core, controllable system, the US accepts a certain degree of credit dilution risk in exchange for systemic stability; in the peripheral, non-US financial systems, it uses stablecoins to expand the dollar's usage and pricing power to hedge against this risk. From this perspective, the Federal Reserve's balance sheet expansion is not a gamble on the perpetual safety of the dollar, but rather an acknowledgment of uncertainty and a proactive adjustment of risk exposure. It is expanding in new markets while under pressure within a controllable range. Mainstream media and institutions interpret today's various phenomena as signals of a dollar decline, ignoring the "supersonic tsunami brought by AI"—(Musk)—that the global financial system is about to face. The path the dollar is taking today is likely the most rational choice a sovereign monetary financial system can make in the face of such massive structural changes. alt="ZSPT3PC8vn3tOzFYdEGQaeLtjZs5WJiNQxJRz1eq.jpeg">

data.title: The Ultimate Deduction on the Value of Creators in the AI ​​Era
data.description:
Author: Haotian; Source: X, @tmel0211

If you, like me, feel helpless about the anxiety surrounding the current market's prevalence of "AI replacing creators," please believe me, this is not just a debate about tools, but a misalignment of cognitive dimensions. Because the vast majority of people's understanding of the relationship between "creation" and "AI" is fundamentally flawed.

They are still debating whether the amount of "AI" in an article determines its quality, completely ignoring the dramatic shift in underlying logic—we are experiencing a great migration from "human consciousness" to "silicon-based carriers."

If you are one of those people who "turns pale at the mention of AI," I have no intention of offending you.

However, I must point out that this conservative mindset is causing you to miss out on the greatest opportunities of this era. Recently, Musk has shown almost insane incentives for creators on the X platform, with some single pieces of content even earning millions of dollars. Why? Is it because they have money to burn? Or is it simply to boost daily active users? If you only see this layer, you're looking too shallowly. Whether you're a classicist still typing away or a pioneer who's already mastered LLM, I hope you'll bookmark this article and take a few minutes to think deeply about it. This isn't just about writing; it's about your ultimate value as a "human being" in this algorithm-dominated future. This will be a profound demystification of the underlying logic of the content industry. What you're rejecting isn't AI, but the fear of your own mediocrity. In this market, people often fall into a binary misconception: either "purely human" craftsmanship or "purely AI" garbage. But this classification is untenable both commercially and logically. First, we must clarify a core axiom: Excellent content never asks about its source. When you're deeply moved by a profound article or struck by a brilliant analysis, do you care whether it was typed word by word by the author or polished by Notion AI? You don't. What you care about is information density, emotional resonance, and cognitive growth. In my view, creators using AI for creation is essentially just an "efficiency improvement activity." This is no different in essence from switching from handwriting to typing, or from researching in libraries to using Google. Those who cling to the "purely manual" standard and derive a sense of superiority from it are often masking their inner anxieties. Subconsciously, they know that **if stripped of the halo of "hard work," their output might not stand up to scrutiny in terms of intellectual depth.** If your thoughts are sharp enough, AI will merely be your exoskeleton, freeing you from tedious textual compilation to construct grander narratives; if your thoughts are barren, AI will indeed replace you, because it can generate ten thousand sentences of mediocre nonsense per second. Whether it was written by AI is not important; what matters is whether there is a "human" thinking behind it. The essence of creators: the greatest "data labelers" in the AI ​​era. If the first point was merely about tool iteration, then this point touches upon the crux of AI evolution. Let's redefine the ecological niche of "creators" in the AI ​​era. Many people think creators are competitors of AI; they are wrong. Creators are the highest-level "data labelers" in the AI ​​evolution chain, engineers who breathe life into raw data. Imagine how LLM (Large Language Model) works. It can learn all the academic papers, humorous novels, and hardcore code in human history in seconds. But to AI, these are merely a cold, probabilistic combination of tokens. AI understands syntax, but not the feeling of heartbreak; AI understands logic, but not the decisiveness of a desperate gamble. This is where the creator comes in. When you take the scattered materials generated by AI, and through your aesthetic sense, your values, and your experience, trim, reorganize, and polish them to create a vivid and compelling article, you are actually doing something truly remarkable: you are infusing silicon-based data with the "soul and consciousness" of carbon-based civilization. This act is essentially a high-level form of data labeling. Every revision you make, every adjustment to the punctuation for better phrasing, every addition for logical consistency—all of these tell the AI, "Hey, this is the kind of expression humans truly like; this is 'human' thinking." Musk relentlessly motivates creators because he knows better than anyone that the real-time, vibrant content on Twitter, brimming with emotion and debate, is the most valuable "sample of human consciousness" for training Grok and future, more sophisticated AI. The Law of Entropy Increase: AI's Hunger and the Curse of Synthetic Data. This leads to a deeper business and technological paradox, and also why "humans" will remain irreplaceable in the future. Current large-scale AI models are already in a state of deep "data hunger." High-quality public data on the internet is rapidly depleting. If humans stop creating, or creators leave en masse, what will AI eat next? It can only consume the data it produces itself. This is akin to inbreeding. If AI relies solely on self-synthetic data to sustain itself, its capabilities will not improve; instead, the accumulation of errors will lead to "model collapse." It's like photocopying a document a thousand times—the result is only a blurry black-and-white mess of noise. This is clearly not the optimal solution, and may even be a fast track to a technological dead end. Therefore, the more widespread and powerful AI becomes, the more urgent its need for "original" and "native" human data becomes. It requires the emergence of creators, it requires people to experience real pain and real joy, and then transform these life experiences that AI cannot simulate into text, video, and audio. This data, containing human "randomness," "creativity," and "emotional tension," is the only antidote to the entropy increase of AI systems. Finally, looking ahead: From "Creators" to "World Builders" So, back to the initial question. Why shouldn't we reject AI, but rather embrace it with extreme greed? Because the rules of the game have changed. In the old era, your ceiling depended on your typing speed and physical strength; in the AI ​​era, your ceiling depends only on your imagination and judgment. The top creators of the future will no longer be mere "wordsmiths." They will evolve into "World Builders." Your thoughts are the blueprints. AI is your construction team. And your work is the independent kingdom you build. Stop worrying about how much "AI flavor" is in your content; that's just part of the process. Think about how to use AI to amplify the density of your thoughts, how to leave a vivid sample of yourself as a unique individual within this algorithm-woven network. After all, in the torrent of silicon-based life, the most expensive offering we can provide is that tiny spark of "humanity," unpredictable by algorithms.

data.title: Freezing billions of dollars in assets: A warning from the Sui consensus disruption incident
data.description:
Introduction: A Network Outage Tests the Maturity of a New Public Chain

Recently, the high-speed Layer 1 blockchain Sui experienced a network outage lasting nearly 6 hours.

This incident resulted in the inability to confirm on-chain transactions, freezing approximately $1 billion in asset activity, marking Sui's second major system-level failure since its mainnet launch in 2023.

Although the network eventually returned to normal operation, and the price of the native token SUI did not experience drastic fluctuations, this event once again brought a long-standing question to the forefront:

Is a high-performance blockchain trading complexity for vulnerability?

II. The Second Major Outage: Sui's First Time Paying Tuition Fees

It's worth noting that this is not the first time Sui has encountered serious network problems.

  • November 2024: Sui experienced a large-scale performance and stability issue

  • This incident in 2025: Another system-level consensus failure

For a Layer 1 network still in a period of rapid expansion, such frequency is not uncommon, but the market's tolerance is decreasing.

Sui is primarily developed by Mysten Labs, whose core team originated from Meta's now-defunct Diem stablecoin project. It belongs to the same technological spectrum as "high-throughput public chains" such as Aptos. Over the past year, Sui's ecosystem growth has been remarkable: 30-day DEX trading volume exceeded $10 billion; institutional attention has increased; 21Shares previously disclosed plans to launch an ETF product tracking SUI. Therefore, the symbolic significance of this shutdown far outweighs its direct economic losses. III. The "Old Problems" of High-Performance Public Chains: Sui, Solana, and System Complexity. Sui's problems are not isolated. Over the past few years, high-throughput blockchains have repeatedly exposed a common risk: When systems become highly complex in pursuit of performance, the stability of the consensus layer becomes even more difficult to guarantee. A typical example is Solana. Solana experienced several long outages in its early days, but has not experienced any major disruptions in the past 18 months. This improvement is primarily due to: More frequent, mandatory validator upgrades; an emergency patching mechanism; and systematic optimization of validator node communication efficiency. Just recently, the Solana team was still urging validators on the X platform to upgrade to the new version containing a "critical patch set" to prevent potential downtime risks. This demonstrates that: High speed ≠ uninterrupted; stability comes from continuous engineering governance, not a one-off design. IV. Beyond Downtime: Is Decentralization Really More Reliable? While news of the Sui outage was spreading, Vitalik Buterin was also publicly discussing another, more macro-level issue. He cited the massive Cloudflare outage in November 2024 as an example, pointing out that centralized internet infrastructure continues to fail frequently. Vitalik emphasized that the long-term value of decentralized applications (DApps) lies in their ability to: * Not rely on single-point infrastructure * Resist censorship and third-party interference * Become a fundamental component of the "world computer" However, the reality is that even blockchain itself is not inherently immune to systemic failures. Decentralization does not automatically equate to high availability. Sui's recent outage serves as a stark illustration of this contradiction. V. Market Reaction: Price Stability Does Not Mean Risk Disappearance From a market perspective, investors reacted relatively restrainedly to this event. According to CoinGecko data:SUI initially rose by about 4% after the outage news broke, then fell back to around $1.84 and fluctuated. In the short term, trading volume increased, but there was no panic selling. This reflects two realities: The market's psychological threshold for "technical outages" is increasing. Investors are focusing more on the long-term ecosystem and team responsiveness, rather than single incidents. However, this doesn't mean the risk has disappeared. For developers, DeFi protocols, and institutional users, predictable stability is often more important than TPS. In conclusion: Sui needs more than just a review; it needs "engineering trust." The Sui network has resumed operation, and users have returned to normal on-chain activities. But the real question is: will it happen again? For a Layer 1 blockchain attempting to support large-scale financial activities, each outage consumes not only time and transaction fees, but also engineering trust. After 2025, the core indicator of public blockchain competition is shifting from "who is faster" to "who is more stable and predictable." Sui's recent consensus interruption may be a necessary price to pay for its maturation. The key is whether it can, like Solana, turn incidents into turning points in engineering evolution, rather than recurring hidden dangers.

data.title: How volatile is the market for prediction?
data.description:
Author: KarenZ, Foresight News

Profit-seeking has evolved into a sophisticated investment. However, in the star-studded prediction market arena, not all projects are worth investing time and money in.

Especially prediction markets, unlike zero-cost testnet tasks, usually require "real money" bets. Although the amount per bet is controllable, this "paid" barrier directly filters out a large number of script-based freeloaders and also brings the risk of principal depletion to real users.

If you are still struggling with which project to bet on in 2026, this article will help you analyze it from three dimensions: reasons for attention, degree of involution (competitive data), and cost-effectiveness of interaction.

Before you rush in, please read this "self-defense" guide: Regulatory risks. Do not participate with funds you cannot afford to lose. Prediction markets are susceptible to bot manipulation, manipulation, and insider trading. Participating in prediction market interactions requires you to have a certain level of judgment regarding news headlines, sports events, or crypto industry dynamics. Carefully review the judgment rules, including the source of information in the contract, whether the deadline includes the current day, and any wording traps. Blindly betting simply to generate interaction will likely result in the loss of your principal. Data from Hubble AI shows that "on Polymarket, the most active retail traders (mid-frequency traders), while having the highest win rate across the network, have a median actual return close to zero due to limitations in capital efficiency and a lack of systemic advantages." Hedging strategies can be tried: Avoid one-sided betting in uncertain prediction markets; try cross-platform hedging. Although there are price spreads and slippage, this minimizes the risk of one-sided betting, allowing you to focus on increasing trading volume. It is important to note that no trading strategy can completely eliminate risk; careful evaluation is necessary before implementation. Why is Polymarket worth paying attention to? Polymarket is a market leader in prediction; it has raised over $2.2 billion in total funding, including a $2 billion strategic investment from Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange. Matthew Modabber, Chief Marketing Officer of Polymarket, previously confirmed the POLY token and airdrop plan in October 2025. Polymarket has received CFTC approval to list in the US and is returning to the US market. Strong expectations are for a token launch and retroactive airdrop in 2026. How far will this go? With nearly 920,000 traders, the user base is enormous. Monthly active users have consistently exceeded 460,000 over the past three months, demonstrating stable user engagement. Weekly trading volume has surpassed $1 billion in recent weeks. The average number of transactions per active wallet per day surged from less than 10 in December 2025 to a peak of 23 in early January. Regarding average betting amounts, 20.69% of users bet less than $10 on average, 30.35% bet between $10 and $50 on average, 23.91% bet between $100 and $500 on average, and 0.24% bet over $10,000 on average. Is it worth interacting with? It's worth participating, but costs need to be strictly controlled. As a high-trading project, its potential airdrop size is extremely attractive. However, Polymarket's mature user ecosystem means that a large number of historical traders have already occupied a portion of the retroactive airdrop quota, and new users need to rationally assess the return on investment. In October 2025, according to Bloomberg, Polymarket was in early-stage talks with investors, planning to raise funds at a valuation of $12-15 billion, more than ten times the valuation four months prior, indicating a continued increase in the project's perceived value. Kalshi has raised nearly $1.6 billion in total. Its most recent Series E funding round occurred in December 2025, raising $1 billion out of $11 billion, with participation from Sequoia, a16z, ARK Invest, Y Combinator, and others. This represents a doubling of the valuation from the $300 million funding round in October 2025.

  • Regulated by the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).

  • Kalshi has not yet officially disclosed any token or airdrop plans. However, based on its recent expansion into the crypto ecosystem, community analysis suggests an airdrop is expected.

    • How far has it gone?

    • Cumulative notional trading volume has reached $30 billion.

    • Since August 2025, notional trading volume has been spiraling upwards, with recent weeks showing a steady growth momentum between $1.5 billion and $2 billion.

    • The total number of transactions is close to 130 million, placing it among the top prediction markets in terms of activity. Is it worth interacting with? Participation should be approached with caution; the cost-effectiveness is low. On one hand, the project lacks a clear timeline for token issuance and airdrops, leading to high uncertainty. On the other hand, Kalshi charges transaction fees, resulting in high costs for manipulating trading volume, and its mature business model has low demand for token incentives, limiting the likelihood of an airdrop. Why is Opinion worth paying attention to? Opinion is one of the fastest-growing prediction markets, with a growth rate significantly outpacing most competitors. Opinion was one of the projects selected for Yzi Labs' Q7 MVB Accelerator program in 2024. In March 2025, Opinion completed a $5 million seed round of funding, led by Yzi Labs. In December, the Opinion team announced that it had secured tens of millions of dollars in further funding. Opinion launched its mainnet in October 2025 and introduced an points system, clearly indicating its airdrop plans. How far will the airdrop go? Since October 2025, Opinion's cumulative notional trading volume has reached $15.3 billion, second only to Polymarket ($44.8 billion) and Kalshi ($30 billion). Opinion's current TVL exceeds $140 million. The cumulative number of traders exceeds 170,000. The number of daily trading users exceeded 40,000 in late December and early January, and has been between 10,000 and 20,000 in the last 10 days. Is it worth interacting with? While the volume is relatively small, the airdrop expectation is clear, and it has strong backing from YZi Labs. It's important to note that a weekly trading volume of over $200 is required to consistently earn weekly ranking points. Points are weighted across multiple dimensions, including limit order liquidity provision, trading volume, and holding time bonuses; a reasonable interaction strategy is necessary. Predict.fun is worth paying attention to. Predict.fun was selected for the second quarter of YZi Labs' EASY Residency incubation program in December 2025. Predict.fun is a native prediction market for BNB Chain incubated and invested by YZi Labs. Its founder, dingaling, faced controversy due to the failure of boop.fun and a "insider trading" scandal, and was criticized by Changpeng Zhao. However, they later reconciled, and the negative impact gradually subsided. Predict.fun integrates with the Venus protocol, allowing users to continuously generate returns while waiting for results after placing bets (applicable to some short-term markets). It features a points system and transparent airdrop predictions. To what extent has it become so popular? The cumulative nominal trading volume is $320 million, and the predicted trading volume exceeds $200 million. The cumulative number of transactions reached 478,000, with 36,000 unique trading users, averaging 13 transactions per user. Since mid-December, daily active users have mostly ranged between 2,000 and 4,000, indicating a relatively small user base. The current TVL is approximately $22.58 million. Is it worth interacting with? Low to medium tiers. Compared to Polymarket, where most bettors and institutions gamble, Predict.fun is mostly used for interaction-based betting. Strategies for accumulating PP points include trading on the platform, providing meaningful liquidity, and holding positions. Why should you pay attention to Probable? Probable is co-incubated by PancakeSwap and YZi Labs, with a legitimate background. Probable will launch its points program on January 12, 2026, but currently only points can be earned through invitations. There are no transaction fees. How much can you participate in? Since December 18, 2025, the cumulative nominal trading volume has reached $560 million, with a projected trading volume of $380 million. The cumulative number of users is 12,389. From late December 2025 to early January 2026, daily active users (DAU) ranged from 500 to 1000. DAU gradually increased starting in early January, with daily DAU exceeding 4000 on January 14th and 15th. The cumulative number of transactions is 834,357, averaging 67 transactions per user. The average transaction size is $425, and the average total transaction size per user is $3357. Is it worth interacting with? Low commission. Probable launched in mid-December and is currently in its early stages. Note that the referrer must first accumulate $100 in transactions to unlock and generate an invitation code. Furthermore, as a project incubated by PancakeSwap, its token will most likely launch through PancakeSwap's CAKE.PAD, and may even be directly listed on Binance.

data.title: 13.6 trillion HTX tokens were burned in a single quarter, bringing the deflationary process close to the critical 10% mark.
data.description:
On January 15th, HTX DAO announced that the Q4 2025 $HTX token burn has been successfully completed. Through the strict implementation of the quarterly burn mechanism, the circulating supply structure of $HTX has been further optimized, and the token's scarcity and value anchor have been gradually strengthened, creating more certain value expectations for ecosystem participants and long-term holders.

Q4 Burn Data Shines: Over 30% Growth Against the Market Trend

On-chain data shows that a total of 13,616,371,495,327.31 $HTX were burned this time, with a total value exceeding $23.31 million.

data.title: Iran's path to de-dollarization: When weapons begin to be settled with cryptocurrencies
data.description: When a country begins accepting cryptocurrency payments for arms orders, crypto assets are no longer simply a matter of "financial innovation" or a "gray market tool," but are formally incorporated into the national survival and international power dynamics. In January 2026, Iran's Ministry of Defense Export Center, Mindex, explicitly stated in official documents that its overseas military contracts could accept cryptocurrency, barter trade, or Iranian rials as payment methods. Arms trade has always been one of the most sanctioned, regulated, and sensitive cross-border transaction scenarios. Iran's decision to publicly include cryptocurrency as a payment option in this area signifies one thing: crypto assets are being systematically used by Iran as a "financial tool to resist sanctions." Driven by Realistic Constraints: Over the past few years, Iran has been under three highly realistic constraints: The local currency, the rial, has been depreciating for a long time, and the foreign exchange system is fragile. The international banking system has been largely cut off. Energy exports and military trade continue to face settlement and delivery risks. Against this backdrop, in 2025, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly stated that without accepting cryptocurrencies, Iran would be unable to achieve its national goal of a 10% digital economy in GDP, and called for the prompt development of a national roadmap for crypto assets. This isn't technological idealism; it's a sober assessment formed under the reality of long-term sanctions—without the introduction of encryption, many economic goals simply cannot be achieved. The world's fourth-largest cryptocurrency mining center. In reality, Iran's dependence on crypto assets is far more radical than its statements suggest. On the one hand, Iran has become the world's fourth-largest cryptocurrency mining center. Thanks to substantial electricity subsidies, even rampant illegal mining has brought it considerable computing power and crypto assets. On the other hand, crypto assets are also deeply embedded in more sensitive areas. Israel's National Counter-Terrorism Financing Authority disclosed that addresses associated with the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) have received approximately $1.5 billion in USDT. While some addresses may belong to exchanges or sharing services, the sheer scale of this number is enough to illustrate that stablecoins are becoming a crucial liquidity vehicle for Iran to circumvent sanctions. A "Firefly" in the Dark In January 2026, due to protests and a currency crisis, Iran implemented a nationwide internet shutdown. This should have been a "fatal blow" to cryptocurrency trading, but the outcome was unexpected. In the absence of internet access, various offline or weak network solutions were quickly discussed and deployed: Starlink satellite network; Blockstream satellite network supporting global broadcasting of Bitcoin data; Bitchat Bluetooth mesh communication tool; Darkwire, a Bitcoin transmission solution without internet access; and Machankura, which supports sending and receiving Bitcoin over telecommunications networks. These solutions are not mature and cannot replace the internet on a large scale, but in such an extreme environment, the crypto industry has demonstrated its remarkable resilience. When traditional communication and financial systems fail simultaneously, crypto assets become a "last resort." The era of "strategic tools" is exemplified by Iran's experience, a microcosm of how nations survive under extreme sanctions. It demonstrates the unique geopolitical value of cryptocurrencies: bypassing traditional financial systems to transfer value and acquire strategic resources. Russia's oil trade, Venezuela's "shadow Bitcoin reserves," and now Iran's arms deals all point to an undeniable reality: cryptocurrencies are evolving from "financial tools" to "geopolitical tools," becoming a new medium connecting national strategies with the global economy.

data.title: Will the US economy rebound like a spring in 2026?
data.description:
Author: BiyaNews

Recently, Cathie Wood, the "Tech Queen" of ARK Invest, released her annual heavyweight outlook letter. While Wall Street is generally filled with concerns about a "rolling recession" and high valuations, Wood paints a completely different picture: the US economy is not at its limit, but rather a "spring compressed to its limit," poised for a V-shaped rebound driven by technological deflation and a productivity explosion in 2026. She even boldly predicts that inflation will not only subside but may even turn negative.

This sounds counterintuitive, right? After all, we've just gone through a rate hike cycle, and market sentiment has been volatile. But Wood's logic is very clear, and she's betting on disruptive technologies that are converging and could reshape everything. Today, we'll dissect this "map of the future" to see what opportunities and risks it holds.

The Tightened "Economic Spring": The Recession is Only Localized. Wood's core argument is that the economy of the past few years was not a widespread overheating, but rather a "rolling recession." The Federal Reserve's aggressive interest rate hikes, like a precise scalpel, suppressed specific sectors, while other parts accumulated enormous rebound potential. Consider the data: Existing home sales have plummeted by about 41% from their 2021 peak and have lingered at low levels for two years, a slump comparable to the early 1980s—when the US population was only 35% of what it is now. The manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) has struggled in contraction territory (below 50) for nearly three years. Meanwhile, consumer confidence among low- and middle-income groups has fallen to multi-decade lows, and recently even confidence among high-income groups has begun to collapse.

Doesn't this resemble a spring being compressed to varying degrees in different parts? Housing, manufacturing, and parts of the consumer sector bore the brunt of the pressure. But on the other hand, a key indicator—spending on non-defense capital goods (excluding aircraft)—after a two-decade-long plateau following the tech bubble, has finally broken through its ceiling, driven by new technologies such as artificial intelligence.

... Wood believes we are standing at the beginning of the strongest capital expenditure cycle in history, and "an AI bubble? She says it will take several more years to form."

Three major "decompression" drivers: the combined effect of policy, inflation, and technology

For a spring to bounce back, it needs to release pressure. Wood points out three decompression valves that will soon work together:

  1. Fiscal and regulatory "loosening": She expects deregulation (especially in the AI ​​and crypto asset sectors) and potential tax cuts to unleash innovation.

  2. Fiscal and regulatory "loosening": She anticipates that deregulation (especially in the AI ​​and crypto asset sectors) and potential tax cuts will unleash innovation.

  3. For example, tax incentives such as accelerated depreciation could push the effective tax rate for US companies to one of the lowest tiers globally, directly benefiting cash flow and capital expenditure. The "Unexpected Downturn" in Inflation: Here her view is the boldest. She believes that inflation may fall to extremely low levels, or even turn negative, in the coming years. Supporting arguments include: oil prices have fallen sharply from their highs; the growth of new and existing home prices has slowed significantly (builders are lowering prices to clear inventory); and most importantly, productivity is improving. In the third quarter of last year, US non-farm productivity increased by 1.9% year-on-year, exceeding the growth in hourly wages, leading to a decrease in the unit labor cost inflation rate to 1.2%. Technology is becoming the most powerful deflationary force.

  1. Technology-driven "productivity explosion": This is the cornerstone of all of Wood's predictions. She believes that the convergence of five major innovation platforms—artificial intelligence, robotics, energy storage, blockchain, and multi-omics sequencing—will drive productivity growth to an annualized rate of 4-6% in the coming years. This will not only suppress inflation but also create enormous wealth. A striking statistic is that, according to some benchmarks, AI inference costs are declining at a rate of up to 99% per year. The collapse of the cost curve will trigger explosive growth in applications.

  1. Technology-driven "productivity explosion": This is the cornerstone of all of Wood's predictions. She believes that the convergence of five major innovation platforms—artificial intelligence, robotics, energy storage, blockchain, and multi-omics sequencing—will drive productivity growth to an annualized rate of 4-6% in the coming years. This will not only suppress inflation but also create enormous wealth. The Chain Reaction of Asset Allocation: Gold, Bitcoin, and the US Dollar If the above macroeconomic projection holds true, then the performance logic of various assets will be rewritten. Wood also offered her insights in her letter: Gold vs. Bitcoin: Last year, gold prices surged while Bitcoin remained relatively weak. An interesting interpretation is "relative scarcity." The rate at which global stock market wealth (measured by the MSCI World Index) is created may exceed the growth rate of gold supply (approximately 1.8% annualized). However, the key is that gold miners can increase production in response to high prices, while Bitcoin's supply is pre-set and completely unmanageable. From an asset allocation perspective, Bitcoin has extremely low correlation with gold and other major asset classes, making it an excellent diversification tool. Will the US Dollar Make a Comeback? Last year, the US dollar index experienced a significant decline, and voices predicting the "end of American exceptionalism" were heard throughout the market. But Wood believes that if her prediction comes true—that the US achieves higher returns on invested capital through technological breakthroughs and policy adjustments—then the dollar will regain its upward trend. She draws a parallel to the near doubling of the dollar in the early days of Reaganomics in the 1980s. Where are the "returns" on AI investment? Current investments in AI infrastructure such as data centers are booming, far exceeding the norm of the past decade. A crucial question is: where will the returns from this massive investment be reflected? Wood points out that, besides hardware giants like Nvidia and cloud service providers, the real growth dividends are flowing to unlisted AI-native companies. Companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are seeing revenues grow at an astonishing rate (tens or even hundreds of times). The future leaders in AI will be those companies that can translate cutting-edge technologies into tangible applications. High Valuations in the Stock Market: Opportunity or Trap? The current valuations of US stocks are undeniably at historical highs, deterring many investors. Wood acknowledges this, but offers a historical perspective: the strongest bull markets in history have often been accompanied by a "compression" of the price-to-earnings ratio (P/E). For example, in the bull markets of the mid-1990s and mid-2000s, the S&P 500 achieved substantial annualized returns, but the P/E ratio declined during the same period. The core driver was that corporate earnings growth outpaced stock price increases. She believes that if productivity-driven real GDP growth accelerates while inflation slows, the current cycle may follow a similar pattern—strong earnings growth digesting or even lowering valuation multiples. This might mean that market opportunities will shift from simple valuation expansion to a deeper exploration of genuine earnings growth and industry disruptors. Cathy Wood's outlook is, as always, bold and systematic. Her predictions are built on the core assumptions of "technological deflation" and a "productivity revolution." If she's right, we may be standing at the beginning of a new era, where growth and investment logic will be reshaped; if she's wrong, the compressed "spring" may lose its elasticity due to other shocks. Regardless, the value of this deduction lies in forcing us to consider long-term forces that transcend short-term fluctuations: How much efficiency can AI actually improve? How can policy resonate with innovation? In an era of collapsing technology cost curves, what assets can truly preserve value? As investors, we need to maintain an open mind, neither blindly optimistic about high valuations nor ignoring the power of disruptive technologies. Markets are always changing, but understanding the underlying logic of change is the best way to cope with uncertainty.

data.title: Iran’s Crypto Boom Swells to $7.8B As Protests Amidst Economic Crisis And Protest
data.description:
Iran Crypto's Popularity Spikes Amid Unrest, Inflation, and Internet Shutdown

Iran’s cryptocurrency ecosystem surged to $7.78 billion in 2025, accelerating sharply during weeks of nationwide protests sparked by economic collapse and political repression, according to new data from blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis.

🇮🇷Iran recorded $7.8 billion in crypto activity as inflation, economic stress, and internet shutdowns pushed people to alternatives.

A large part of the inflow came from state-linked actors facing sanctions. pic.twitter.com/2fLB8TngPT

— Bitinning (@bitinning) January 16, 2026

The spike coincided with mass demonstrations that erupted in late December after the Iranian rial plunged to record lows against the U.S. dollar, deepening an already severe cost-of-living crisis. As unrest spread, authorities responded with internet shutdowns and forceful crackdowns, pushing many Iranians to seek alternatives to the traditional financial system.

In that environment, Bitcoin emerged as both a store of value and an escape hatch, offering liquidity and portability at a time when banks, currency controls and state oversight became liabilities rather than safeguards.

Chainalysis found a pronounced increase in daily crypto transfers and transaction volumes during the unrest, with the most revealing signal coming from exchange activity. Large numbers of users withdrew Bitcoin from Iranian exchanges into unattributed personal wallets, a move analysts interpret as a rush toward self-custody.

In the last 12 hours, Iran has experienced significant civil unrest, with a total of 385 reports, including 264 protests and 8 clashes. The protests are largely driven by public discontent and have been met with a notable police presence, particularly in urban areas. The internet… pic.twitter.com/Y8ELobIEsR

— nkl (@NavidKianiL) January 16, 2026

Chainalysis said, noting that Iranians were taking direct possession of Bitcoin at a far higher rate during protests than before

“Most telling is the surge in withdrawals from Iranian exchanges to personal Bitcoin wallets.”

The behavior, the firm added, reflects a rational response to the collapse of the rial, which has lost so much value that it is now effectively unusable against major global currencies.

Experts say this shift highlights growing distrust in centralized institutions during periods of crisis. With capital controls tightening and inflation eroding savings, Bitcoin’s fixed supply and censorship-resistant design have made it an increasingly attractive hedge against both monetary debasement and political risk.

Chainalysis reports a surge in Bitcoin buying in Iran as the rial rapidly loses value.

After falling from 42 to 1,050 per USD in a matter of weeks, the currency has effectively collapsed, pushing residents to use #BTC to preserve savings and move funds abroad outside the banking… pic.twitter.com/1viSgyTuwk

— BTC Live (@btcliveco) January 16, 2026

Crypto as resistance — and a regime tool

While ordinary Iranians are turning to crypto to preserve wealth and bypass restrictions, Chainalysis also observed a striking paradox: the Iranian state is doing the same.

Addresses linked to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) accounted for roughly half of Iran’s total crypto activity in the fourth quarter of 2025, with more than $3 billion received over the year. The data underscores how digital assets have become embedded on both sides of Iran’s economic divide — used by citizens seeking autonomy and by state-linked actors navigating sanctions and isolation.

According to Chainalysis, Bitcoin’s appeal in this context goes beyond price appreciation. Its self-custodial and censorship-resistant nature offers flexibility in environments where traditional assets can be frozen, seized or rendered inaccessible overnight.

Iran is not an outlier. Chainalysis noted that regions facing war, economic collapse or government crackdowns consistently show similar spikes in Bitcoin withdrawals during periods of instability.

Despite a blanket ban, protesters in Nepal are using crypto to dodge bank restrictions and surveillance pic.twitter.com/ktwBZ9VGRn

— Neeraj K. Agrawal (@NeerajKA) September 12, 2025

In Iran’s case, the trend is reinforced by scale. TRM Labs estimates that roughly $3.7 billion in crypto flows moved through the country between January and July 2025, while data from Statista suggests that around seven million Iranians — nearly 8% of the population — are now crypto users. Persistent sanctions pressure and limited access to global financial rails have only accelerated adoption.

Crypto’s role in a fractured economy

As sanctions intensify and the rial continues its downward spiral, analysts believe cryptocurrency will remain a critical tool for financial sovereignty in Iran. Bitcoin’s liquidity, portability and independence from government control make it uniquely suited for populations facing systemic instability — especially when individuals may need to move funds quickly or operate outside official channels.

Chainalysis concluded that crypto’s role in Iran is unlikely to fade once the protests subside. Instead, digital assets appear increasingly woven into the country’s economic reality, serving as both a hedge against collapse and a parallel financial system in an environment where trust in traditional structures has eroded.

data.title: From Cash to Crypto to Prison: Utah Man Sentenced for Multi-Million Dollar Fraud
data.description:
Operating from a quiet corner of Washington County, Brian Garry Sewell appeared to be a high-flying expert in the digital gold rush.

To his students and investors, he was a dual-degree scholar from Stanford and Johns Hopkins with the Midas touch for cryptocurrency.

In reality, the 54-year-old was a GED holder weaving a multi-million-pound web of lies that has now culminated in a three-year federal prison sentence.

The judgment, handed down on 15 January 2026, marks the end of a sprawling enforcement effort that saw the Department of Justice pursue Sewell for both blatant investment fraud and the shadowy operation of an unlicensed money-moving business.

Southern Utah Man Sentenced to Three Years in Prison for Wire Fraud that Cost Investors Millions of Dollars

PRESS RELEASE: 🔗https://t.co/gWgh0bA0W0pic.twitter.com/ImDRq7i2rM

— US Attorney Utah (@USAO_UT) January 15, 2026

The Fiction Of A High Yield Crypto Hedge Fund

Between December 2017 and April 2024, Sewell orchestrated a scheme that preyed on the aspirations of 17 individual investors.

He claimed to run a sophisticated hedge fund, the Rockwell Fund, which he promised would use advanced strategies to deliver massive returns with negligible risk.

To build trust, he fabricated an elite educational background and a history of successful fund management.

The deception worked, allowing Sewell to amass more than $2.9 million in investor capital.

📰 Breaking News: Crypto teacher's costly lesson – $1.2M student investment gone in fake hedge fund scam! 🚨 Beware !!!#CryptoScams#InvestmentWarning#CryptoFraud"https://t.co/3Tq8AxeCaC

— CryptoIdea (@cryptoideatoken) February 3, 2024

However, the promised trading never happened.

Instead of managing a high-tech fund, Sewell simply held the funds in Bitcoin.

The scheme took a final, disastrous turn when Sewell claimed his digital wallet was hacked and the assets looted, leaving his victims with nothing.

Robert Bohls, Special Agent in Charge of the Salt Lake City FBI, said in an official statement,

"Sewell preyed on his victims by lying about his experience and promising returns he could not deliver, leaving individuals and families to bear the consequences of his deception.”

Processing Millions For The Criminal Underworld

While the investment fraud was unfolding, Sewell was also running a parallel "cash-to-crypto" operation through his company, Rockwell Capital Management.

From March to September 2020, he acted as a high-volume, unlicensed money transmitter, converting bulk cash into digital assets for a fee.

Source: Linkedin

Federal prosecutors revealed that this business moved more than $5.4 million (£4 million) for third-party clients, including individuals involved in drug trafficking and large-scale fraud.

By ignoring mandatory anti-money laundering (AML) registrations and reporting requirements, Sewell provided a critical service for criminals looking to disguise the origins of their illicit wealth.

Federal Authorities Signal No Safe Harbour

The dual cases against Sewell resulted in a total restitution order of $3,822,909.

This includes over $3.6 million for defrauded investors, a mortgage lender, and a credit union, along with $217,727 destined for the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.

His two three-year prison terms will run concurrently, followed by 36 months of supervised release.

Legal experts suggest that this prosecution in Utah indicates a broader strategy by the Department of Justice to target regional operators, not just the industry's biggest names.

By using unlicensed money transmission charges as a "fail-safe," prosecutors can secure convictions based on the illegality of the operation itself, regardless of whether a jury finds specific fraudulent intent.

A Record Year For Global Crypto Crime

Sewell’s sentencing arrives as the scale of digital asset misuse reaches unprecedented heights.

According to data from Chainalysis, 2025 was the most volatile year on record for illicit activity, with criminal addresses receiving a staggering $154 billion (£114 billion).

This represents a 162% surge compared to the revised $57.2 billion reported in 2024.

In the introductory chapter to our 2026 Crypto Crime Report, we reveal that illicit cryptocurrency transactions received at least $154 billion in 2025 (a 162% YoY increase). Nation-state activity and sanctions evasion drove the surge, marking a new phase in crypto crime. Read… pic.twitter.com/gedfxuDgUs

— Chainalysis (@chainalysis) January 8, 2026

The rise is largely attributed to the professionalisation of on-chain infrastructure, where illicit organisations now provide "laundering-as-a-service" to transnational networks.

As authorities increase their focus on both large exchanges and individual "cash-out" hubs like Sewell’s, the gap between the regulated financial world and the digital underground continues to shrink.
